rulings.Case Title: WILLIAM PEARSON VS. ASBESTOS DEFENDANTS (B/P)AS REFLECTED ON EXHIBITS et al

rulings.Case Title: MICHAEL STEINBERGER VS. ASBESTOS DEFENDANTS (B/P)AS REFLECTED ON EXHIBITS et al
rulings.Rulings: On Asbestos Law and Motion Calendar for Thursday, September 23, 2010 in Department 220 at 9:30 a.m., Line 3. Defendant Metalclad Insulation Corp.¿s motion for summary judgment is off calendar per its counsel¿s September 17 letter that Metalclad is no longer seeking adjudication of the failure to warn claims. Metalclad¿s alternate motion for summary adjudication is granted as to issues 1 (negligence excluding failure to warn) and 3 (strict liability design and manufacturing defect) and taken of calendar as to the remaining issues per Metalclad¿s request. The admissible evidence establishes that Metalclad is entitled to judgment in its favor on the non-failure to warn negligence and strict liability claims based on the government contractor defense. On the court¿s own motion the hearing is continued to Monday September 27 at 9am. Anyone wishing to contest the tentative ruling must do so by 4:30pm today September 22. =(220/HEK)

rulings.Case Title: ROBERT MONTOGMERY VS. A.W. CHESTERTON COMPANY et al
rulings.Rulings: On Asbestos Law and Motion Calendar for Thursday, September 23, 2010 in Department 220 at 9:30 a.m., Line 4. Defendant Elementis Chemicals Inc.¿s motion for summary judgment is denied. The deposition testimony of Mr. Hamilton, Mr. Persson and Ms. Sultz create a triable issue whether Robert Montgomery was exposed to an asbestos-containing product or material (Hamilton joint compound and/or raw Union Carbide Calidria) attributable to defendant. The bulk supplier defense is not available to a supplier of raw asbestos in asbestos exposure cases because asbestos is inherently dangerous and raw asbestos fibers do not change when they become a component of an asbestos-containing product. (Garza; Jenkins; Arena). =(220/HEK)

rulings.Case Title: ROBERT MONTOGMERY VS. A.W. CHESTERTON COMPANY et al
rulings.Rulings: On Asbestos Law and Motion Calendar for Thursday, September 23, 2010 in Department 220 at 9:30 a.m., Line 4. Defendant Omega Products International Inc.¿s motion for summary judgment is granted. No opposition filed. =(220/HEK)

rulings.Case Title: ROBERT MONTOGMERY VS. A.W. CHESTERTON COMPANY et al
rulings.Rulings: On Asbestos Law and Motion Calendar for Thursday, September 23, 2010 in Department 220 at 9:30 a.m., Line 4. Defendant Highland Stucco & Lime Products, Inc.¿s motion for summary judgment is denied. The deposition testimony of Michael Montgomery and defendant¿s GO 129 interrogatory responses create a triable issue whether Robert Montgomery was exposed to an asbestos-containing product (Highland stucco) attributable to defendant. The objections to defendant¿s GO 129 interrogatory responses and Michael Montgomery¿s deposition testimony are overruled. =(220/HEK)

rulings.Case Title: ROBERT MONTOGMERY VS. A.W. CHESTERTON COMPANY et al
rulings.Rulings: On Asbestos Law and Motion Calendar for Thursday, September 23, 2010 in Department 220 at 9:30 a.m., Line 4. Defendant Merlex Stucco, Inc.¿s motion for summary judgment is granted. Defendant sustained its burden of production and plaintiffs failed to present evidence that Mr. Montgomery was exposed to an asbestos-containing product attributable to defendant. =(220/HEK)

rulings.Case Title: ROBERT MONTOGMERY VS. A.W. CHESTERTON COMPANY et al
rulings.Rulings: On Asbestos Law and Motion Calendar for Thursday, September 23, 2010 in Department 220 at 9:30 a.m., Line 4. Defendant CertainTeed Corp.¿s motion for summary judgment is denied and its alternate motion for summary adjudication is denied as to issues 1, 2 and 3 (negligence, strict liability and survival) and granted as to issue 4 (punitive damages). The deposition testimony or Ms. Sultz creates a triable issue whether Robert Montgomery was exposed to an asbestos-containing product (shingles and felts) attributable to defendant. The objections to Ms. Sultz¿s testimony are overruled. Defendant satisfied its burden of production and plaintiffs did not present clear and convincing evidence that defendant acted with malice, fraud or oppression. =(220/HEK)

Metalclad¿s alternate motion for summary adjudication is granted as to issues 1 (negligence excluding failure to warn) and 3 (strict liability design and manufacturing defect) and taken of calendar as to the remaining issues per Metalclad¿s request. rulings.Case Title: ALLISON FISH et al VS. A.W. CHESTERTON COMPANY et al
rulings.Rulings: On Asbestos Law and Motion Calendar for Thursday, September 23, 2010 in Department 220 at 9:30 a.m., Line 6. Defendant Highland Stucco and Lime Products, Inc.¿s motion for summary judgment is denied. The deposition testimony and declaration of Richard Willoughby and the deposition testimony of Mr. Atkinson create a triable issue whether Paul Willoughby was exposed to asbestos-containing Highland finish coat stucco. Defendant¿s objections to Richard Willoughby¿s declaration and Mr. Atkinson¿s deposition testimony are overruled. =(220/HEK)

rulings.Case Title: MILTON GODBEE VS. ASBESTOS DEFENDANTS (B*P) AS REFLECTED ON EXHIBITS et al
rulings.Rulings: On Asbestos Law and Motion Calendar for Thursday, September 23, 2010 in Department 220 at 9:30 a.m., Line 7. Defendant Marc Paul, Inc.¿s motion for summary judgment is denied. Defendant did not satisfy its burden of production that plaintiff does not possess and cannot reasonably obtain evidence that Mr. Godbee was exposed to an asbestos-containing product (Kaiser Gypsum drywall products) attributable to defendant. Plaintiff¿s interrogatory responses and deposition testimony preclude defendant from invoking the factually devoid prong of Aguilar. =(220/HEK)

rulings.Case Title: CARL WAGNER et al VS. ASBESTOS DEFENDANTS (BP) AS REFLECTED ON EXHIBITS et al
rulings.Rulings: On Asbestos Law and Motion Calendar for Thursday, September 23, 2010 in Department 220 at 9:30 a.m., Line 11. Defendant DAP, Inc.¿s motion for summary judgment is denied and its alternate motion for summary adjudication is denied as to issues 1 (negligence and strict liability) and 3 (loss of consortium) and taken off calendar as to issues 2 (false representation) and 4 (punitive damages). Defendant did not satisfy its burden of production that plaintiffs do not possess and cannot reasonably obtain evidence that Mr. Wagner was exposed to asbestos-containing DAP 33 glazing compound. Mr. Wagner¿s deposition testimony and defendant¿s GO 129 interrogatory responses preclude defendant from invoking the factually devoid prong of Aguilar. Even if defendant had sustained its burden of production, the declaration of Mr. Wagner creates a triable fact regarding his exposure to an asbestos-containing product. The objections to Mr. Wagner¿s declaration are overruled. Plaintiffs dismissed their claims for false representation and punitive damages. =(220/HEK)
